notes:
Note page cleanup — removes legacy header chrome and adds the empty-note
"who links here" landing (Note Build Spec v1).

**New**

- An **empty note that's already linked from elsewhere** now opens straight to
  its **"Mentioned in N notes"** — you land on who points at it, ready to write.
  Start typing and it returns to a normal note. A brand-new empty note with no
  links shows a calm writing prompt instead.

**Fixes & polish**

- The note header is simplified: a single **Pin** plus a **⋯ menu** (Move to
  folder · Open in new tab · Copy link · Archive · Delete). The old duplicate
  **star** and the inline **"Folder ▾ · Archive · Move to Trash"** row are gone;
  the folder now shows in the line under the title.
- That subline now reads **Edited … · in {Folder} · {N} mentions**, and the
  mention count is a tidy chip that jumps to the mentions (no more underlined link).
- The journal drops its redundant per-day subline; each day's mentions sit
  directly under the day with no extra divider.
